description_long: This saxophone features a classic, elongated S-shaped brass body with a prominent, flared bell at the lower end and a gently curved neck leading to the mouthpiece. The surface is highly polished, giving it a glossy, reflective golden-brown finish typical of lacquered brass instruments. Numerous round keys and pads are distributed along the main tube, with some side keys visible, all connected by intricate rods and levers. The bell is wide and smoothly flared, with a dark, open interior and a rounded rim. The neck curves smoothly upward and outward, seamlessly joining the main body. No visible engravings or decorative etchings are present on the exterior, and the mouthpiece appears to be attached.
description: A polished, golden-brown saxophone with a flared bell, curved neck, and a full set of keys and pads along its glossy brass body.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full S-shaped profile, flared bell, and the arrangement of keys and rods along the body.
description_view_1: Narrow edge-on view highlighting the slimness of the body and the alignment of the keys, with the bell and neck mostly in profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view revealing the bell's opening, the curvature of the neck, and the distribution of keys and pads.
description_view_3: Front view looking directly into the bell, showing the circular opening and the alignment of the main tube and neck.
attribute_bell_opening: The bell opening is circular, flared, and oriented outward and slightly upward, with an estimated diameter of about 12 centimeters. The rim is smooth and rounded, with no visible unique features.
attribute_key_layout: The saxophone has a full set of round keys and pads distributed along the main tube, with several side keys visible. The keywork appears standard, with no distinctive or missing keys.
attribute_neck_curve: The neck is smoothly curved, forming a gentle upward and outward arc from the main body. The curvature is continuous and appears to be detachable, as is typical for saxophones.
